notes: Ports derived directly from the official Extron specification sheet (5628-D6, revised 2022). The IPL Pro CR88 has no RS-232, USB, IR, or audio ports — it is exclusively a contact-closure-input/relay-output control processor. The two contact input connectors are 5-pole 3.5mm captive screw blocks (4 inputs + 1 ground each); the four relay connectors are 4-pole 3.5mm captive screw blocks (2 relays + 2 grounds each). The power connector physical form is confirmed as a DC barrel jack by Extron IPL Pro series documentation; exact barrel dimensions (likely 2.1mm) not explicitly stated in the spec sheet. The device is 1U-high quarter-rack-wide and rack-mountable with optional shelf accessory. PoE (802.3af class 3) via the LAN port eliminates need for the external power supply.

ports.name: LAN/PoE
ports.note: 10/100/1000Base-T with autodetect; also accepts PoE (802.3af class 3) as power source

ports.name: Contact In 1-4
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 5-pole (pins 1-4 = inputs 1-4, pin G = ground); one of two connectors providing 8 total contact closure inputs

ports.name: Contact In 5-8
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 5-pole (pins 1-4 = inputs 5-8, pin G = ground); second of two contact closure connectors

ports.name: Relay 1-2
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 4-pole; one of four relay connectors providing 8 total normally-open relays rated 24 VDC, 1 A

ports.name: Relay 3-4
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 4-pole; second relay connector

ports.name: Relay 5-6
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 4-pole; third relay connector

ports.name: Relay 7-8
ports.note: 3.5 mm captive screw, 4-pole; fourth relay connector

ports.name: 12V Power
ports.note: 12 VDC, 1 A max (12 W); powered by included external 100-240 VAC adapter (output 12 VDC, 0.5 A). Device can also be powered via PoE on LAN port instead.
